definition: A thing that differs from what is normal or expected; a departure from a moral standard.
example: The teacher said the student's rude outburst was an aberration, since she was usually polite.

definition: Of the most miserable or degrading kind; showing complete lack of pride or dignity.
example: Living in abject poverty, the family could barely afford one meal a day.

definition: Give or grant someone power or recognition; an official agreement or treaty.
example: The two countries signed an accord to end the long dispute over the border.

definition: Warn or reprimand someone firmly; urge someone earnestly to do or not to do something.
example: The coach admonished the players to always play fairly and respect the referee.

definition: Become less intense or widespread; lessen in force or intensity.
example: After three days of heavy rain, the storm finally began to abate.

definition: A state of temporary disuse or suspension.
example: The project was held in abeyance until more funding could be secured.

definition: A deep or seemingly bottomless chasm; a wide or profound difference between people.
example: Standing at the cliff edge, she stared into the dark abyss below.

definition: Relating to astronomy; extremely large; too large to be estimated.
example: The cost of building a space station is astronomical — billions of dollars.

definition: A short amusing or interesting story about a real incident or person.
example: Grandpa always had a funny anecdote about his school days to share.

definition: Renounce one's throne; fail to fulfil a responsibility or duty.
example: The king chose to abdicate his throne so he could marry the woman he loved.

definition: Sharp-tasting; slightly sour; bitter or cutting.
example: The acidulous lemon juice made her pucker her lips.

definition: Approach and address someone boldly or aggressively.
example: A street vendor accosted us the moment we stepped out of the hotel.

definition: A person who lives an unconventional life, especially as an artist or writer.
example: The bohemian artist decorated her studio with colourful murals and odd sculptures.

definition: Wet muddy ground that is too soft to support a heavy body.
example: The hikers got their boots stuck when they wandered into the bog.

definition: Support or strengthen; a long firm pillow placed across a bed.
example: More practice will bolster her confidence before the spelling competition.

definition: Threatening harm; having a harmful or destructive effect; menacing.
example: The villain shot a baleful glare at the hero across the room.

definition: To confuse or make someone unable to think clearly.
example: The complicated instructions befuddled even the most experienced players.

definition: A huge or monstrous creature; something enormous, especially a large and powerful organisation.
example: The new tech company quickly became a behemoth, overtaking all its rivals.

definition: A sharp projection near the end of an arrow or fishhook; a wounding remark.
example: His witty barbs during the debate left his opponent speechless.

definition: Exchanging remarks in a good-humoured teasing way; playful and witty conversation.
example: The two friends were bantering all afternoon, making everyone around them laugh.

definition: Gentle and kindly; of a disease not harmful in effect; not malignant.
example: The doctor confirmed that the lump was benign and nothing to worry about.

definition: Argue about petty and trivial matters; quarrel in a petty manner.
example: The siblings would bicker endlessly about which TV show to watch.

definition: Withdraw from commercial or social relations as punishment or protest; refuse to buy or use.
example: The students decided to boycott the cafeteria to protest the poor food quality.

definition: Well-meaning and kindly; charitable rather than profit making.
example: The benevolent millionaire donated half his fortune to build schools in poor areas.

definition: Of or relating to cattle; slow-moving, dull, or stupid.
example: His bovine expression showed he had no idea what the joke meant.

definition: A dead human body, especially one used for dissection in a medical school.
example: Medical students studied the cadaver carefully to understand human anatomy.

definition: Pleasing or liked on account of having qualities or interests in common; suited to one's taste.
example: She found the new city very congenial as the people were warm and welcoming.

definition: Having the potential to cause cancer; relating to a substance that causes cancer.
example: Doctors warn that smoking is carcinogenic and greatly increases the risk of lung cancer.

definition: Express severe disapproval of; an official reprimand.
example: The committee voted to censure the official who had misused public funds.

definition: The killing of large numbers of people; scenes of terrible destruction.
example: The battlefield was a scene of carnage after the long and brutal conflict.

definition: Positioned in or relating to the sky or outer space; belonging to heaven.
example: The astronomer spent nights mapping the celestial objects visible from the observatory.

definition: Moving or tending to move away from a centre.
example: Centrifugal force keeps water pressed against the sides of a spinning drum.

definition: A small close-knit group of people who do not readily allow others to join them.
example: The popular clique sat together at lunch and rarely invited anyone new.

definition: Causing or likely to cause an argument; involving a heated argument.
example: Whether homework should be banned is a contentious topic in most schools.

definition: Neat and trim in dress and appearance.
example: He arrived at the ceremony looking dapper in a pressed white shirt and blazer.

definition: Delicately small and pretty; fastidious about eating or other activities.
example: She picked up the dainty china teacup with two careful fingers.

definition: A mark against someone's record for a fault or offence.
example: He received a demerit for arriving late to class three days in a row.

definition: Move slowly and idly; take one's time; waste time.
example: Stop dawdling and get ready as the bus leaves in five minutes.

definition: A low platform for a lectern, seats of honour, or a throne.
example: The principal stood on the dais and addressed the entire school.

definition: Scattered pieces of rubbish or remains; loose natural material consisting of rock fragments.
example: After the storm, the streets were littered with fallen branches and debris.

definition: A person who expects and accepts failure; excessively ready to accept defeat.
example: Be positive and keep trying — only a defeatist gives up before even starting.

definition: Demonstrating skill and cleverness; quick and neat in movement.
example: With a deft flick of her wrist, she tied the bow perfectly.

definition: An acutely disturbed state of mind with restlessness and incoherence; wild excitement.
example: The crowd was in a state of delirium when their team scored the winning goal.

definition: Cause to lose determination or confidence; discourage.
example: Losing the first round should not dishearten you — keep trying and improving.

definition: Distracted or absent-minded, especially because of worry or anxiety.
example: She seemed distrait during class, gazing out of the window instead of listening.

definition: Ready to accept control or instruction; submissive; easy to manage.
example: The normally lively puppy became docile after its long walk in the park.

definition: Diminish gradually in size, amount, or strength.
example: As winter arrived, the daylight hours began to dwindle.

definition: Evoke or draw out a response, answer, or fact from someone.
example: The teacher's clever question elicited a surprisingly thoughtful answer from the student.

definition: Unconventional and slightly strange; not following the usual pattern.
example: The eccentric inventor filled his house with robots and spinning contraptions.

definition: An overwhelming feeling of great happiness or joyful excitement.
example: She was in pure ecstasy when she heard her name called as the winner.

definition: Make corrections and improvements to a text.
example: The editor emended several factual errors before the article went to print.

definition: Make more attractive by adding ornamental features; make a story more interesting.
example: He had a habit of embellishing his stories to make them more exciting.

definition: Hold someone closely in one's arms; accept or support willingly.
example: She chose to embrace change rather than fear it.

definition: Of a disease or condition regularly found among particular people or in a certain area.
example: Malaria is endemic in many tropical regions of Africa.

definition: Declare one's public approval or support of something.
example: The famous athlete endorsed the sports drink by appearing in its advertisements.

definition: Tending to avoid commitment or self-revelation; evading or escaping.
example: His evasive answers made the interviewer suspect he was hiding something.

definition: The ability to predict what will happen or be needed in the future.
example: Her foresight in saving money meant she was prepared when the car broke down.

definition: Exhausted; tired out after great exertion.
example: After a full day of rehearsals, the dancers were completely fagged.

definition: A person who has been convicted of a felony.
example: The felon was sentenced to five years in prison for robbery.

definition: The state or quality of being ferocious; savage fierceness.
example: The lion defended its cubs with ferocity.

definition: Search tenaciously for something.
example: The detective spent hours trying to ferret out the truth from the conflicting reports.

definition: Faithfulness to a person, cause, or belief.
example: The soldier pledged his fidelity to his country and its people.

definition: Changing frequently, especially regarding one's loyalties, interests, or affections.
example: Her fickle taste in music meant her playlist changed completely every week.

definition: Incapable of producing any useful result; pointless.
example: It was futile to argue — the decision had already been made.

definition: Very cold in temperature; stiff, formal, and unfriendly in manner.
example: The explorer battled through frigid temperatures to reach the South Pole.

definition: A person's manner of walking; any of the paces of an animal such as a canter or trot.
example: You could recognise her by her light bouncy gait from far away.

definition: Gather or collect; a granary or store.
example: The young actress hoped to garner praise for her debut performance.

definition: Unsophisticated and socially awkward; lacking ease or grace.
example: He made a gauche remark at dinner that made everyone uncomfortable.

definition: In a careful or cautious manner so as to avoid harm or risk.
example: She gingerly picked up the fragile antique vase, afraid it might shatter.

definition: Cover with a glassy coating; fit panes of glass into a window frame; lose brightness.
example: The baker used sugar icing to glaze the freshly baked doughnuts.

definition: A category of artistic composition characterised by similarities in form, style, or subject matter.
example: Mystery is her favourite genre — she has read over fifty detective novels.

definition: Fluent in speaking, but insincere and shallow.
example: The politician gave a glib answer that avoided all the difficult questions.

definition: An alphabetical list of words relating to a specific subject, with explanations.
example: The textbook had a helpful glossary at the back explaining scientific terms.

definition: Dispute or bargain persistently, especially over the cost of something.
example: At the market, he happily haggled over the price of the handmade rug.

definition: The organic component of soil, formed by the decomposition of leaves and other plant material.
example: Gardeners add humus to the soil to help plants grow strong and healthy.

definition: A small squalid or simply constructed dwelling.
example: The old photograph showed a tiny hovel where ten people once lived.

definition: Hostile behaviour; unfriendliness or opposition; an act of warfare.
example: There was open hostility between the two rival football clubs.

definition: A modest or low view of one's own importance; humbleness.
example: Despite winning every award, she spoke with great humility.

definition: Supposed but not necessarily real or true.
example: Consider a hypothetical situation where school lasts only three days a week.

definition: Delay or prevent by obstructing; hinder the progress of.
example: The heavy snowfall began to impede traffic on the main road.

definition: Not feeling or showing emotion; calm; serene.
example: The judge remained impassive throughout the noisy courtroom debate.

definition: Having one's true identity concealed.
example: The famous singer went incognito with a hat and sunglasses to avoid fans.

definition: The action of encrusting or the state of being encrusted; a hard outer coating.
example: Years of incrustation on the ancient statue made it difficult to see the details.

definition: Speak or write about something with great hostility; rail against.
example: The journalist inveighed against corruption in a powerful editorial.

definition: Beyond criticism; faultless; blameless.
example: Her irreproachable behaviour earned her the trust of everyone at school.

definition: The ninth letter of the Greek alphabet; an extremely small amount.
example: She did not care one iota what others thought of her unique sense of style.

definition: Cite or appeal to as an authority; call on a deity in prayer; give rise to; evoke.
example: The lawyer chose to invoke her client's right to remain silent.

definition: Having a cheerful, lively, and self-confident manner; smart and tidy.
example: He walked into the room with a jaunty step, clearly excited about the good news.

definition: Be in accord; agree.
example: Her account of the event does not quite jibe with what the other witnesses said.

definition: Cheerful and friendly; convivial.
example: Our jovial neighbour always made everyone laugh at his stories.

definition: Highly intricate or difficult to resolve.
example: The lawyer had to untangle a knotty legal problem that had confused everyone.

definition: A small hill or mound.
example: The children flew their kites from the grassy knoll at the edge of the park.

definition: Lose or lack vitality; grow weak; be kept in an unpleasant situation for a long time.
example: Without water, the flowers began to languish and droop.

definition: Something that is complex and confusing; an intricate maze.
example: The ancient castle was a labyrinth of narrow corridors and hidden rooms.

definition: Bestow something in generous or extravagant quantities.
example: The hosts put on a lavish feast for their guests with dozens of dishes.

definition: The amount of freedom to move or act.
example: The teacher gave students some leeway in choosing their essay topics.

definition: Furiously angry; of a bluish leaden colour.
example: Mum was livid when she discovered someone had eaten her birthday cake.

definition: Tending to talk a great deal; talkative; garrulous.
example: Her loquacious uncle dominated every conversation at family dinners.

definition: A person who exaggerates or pretends to be ill in order to escape duty or work.
example: The coach suspected the player was a malingerer after he missed three straight practices.

definition: A pale purple colour; a pale bluish-purple dye or colour.
example: She painted her bedroom walls a soft mauve that looked lovely in the evening light.

definition: Sweet or musical; pleasant to hear; flowing sweetly.
example: The singer's mellifluous voice filled the hall and brought the audience to tears.

definition: An optical illusion caused by atmospheric conditions; something illusory and unattainable.
example: Stranded in the desert, the lost traveller was tricked by a mirage of an oasis.

definition: A countless or extremely great number of people or things; innumerable.
example: There were a myriad of stars visible on the clear night sky.

definition: Unpleasantly warm and humid.
example: The muggy summer afternoon made everyone reluctant to go outside.

definition: Appease the anger or anxiety of someone; reduce the severity or violence of something.
example: She tried to mollify her upset friend with a sincere apology.

definition: In the form of a cloud or haze; hazy; vague; ill-defined.
example: His plans for the future were still rather nebulous — he had not decided on anything.

definition: Living the life of a nomad; wandering from place to place.
example: The nomadic tribe moved their camp every season in search of fresh pastures.

definition: Smart and fashionable; cleverly executed.
example: She always arrived looking natty — never a hair out of place.

definition: The quality of being new, original, or unusual; a new or unfamiliar thing or experience.
example: The novelty of the new toy wore off after just a few days.

definition: A person who is new to a subject or activity; a novice.
example: As a neophyte chess player, she was quickly beaten by the club champion.

definition: To look at something with strong interest, often in a way that is inappropriate.
example: The tourists ogled the famous painting, crowding around it to take photos.

definition: Stubbornly refusing to change one's opinion or chosen course of action.
example: He was so obstinate that no amount of logical reasoning could change his mind.

definition: Old-fashioned; no longer current or in fashion.
example: The outmoded computer was so slow it could barely run the latest software.

definition: A person who takes advantage of opportunities as and when they arise, regardless of planning or principle.
example: He was an opportunist who took credit for his colleague's hard work.

definition: Exclude from a society or group.
example: The child was ostracized by the class for telling the teacher about the cheating.

definition: Harsh criticism or censure; the public disgrace arising from shameful conduct.
example: The corrupt official faced public opprobrium after the scandal was exposed.

definition: A piece of legislation enacted by a municipal authority; a religious rite.
example: The city council passed a new ordinance banning the use of plastic bags.

definition: Characterised by unsystematic partial measures taken over a period of time; one piece at a time.
example: The building was renovated piecemeal over ten years rather than all at once.

definition: Having a pleasantly sharp or appetising flavour; pleasantly stimulating to the mind.
example: The chef prepared a piquant sauce that gave the dish a wonderful zing.

definition: A special right or privilege enjoyed as a result of one's position.
example: A company car was one of the perquisites that came with his senior position.

definition: A hidden or unsuspected danger or difficulty; a covered pit for trapping animals.
example: The teacher warned students about the pitfalls of copying work from the internet.

definition: Relating to or tending to cause infectious disease; harmful or dangerous.
example: Mosquitoes can be pestilential in tropical climates, spreading serious diseases.

definition: Of or involving strongly critical or controversial writing or speech.
example: Her polemical article about school uniforms sparked a fierce debate online.

definition: Tending to propel; relating to the action of propelling.
example: The rocket's propulsive force carried it far beyond the atmosphere.

definition: Failing to observe the limits of what is permitted or appropriate; bold.
example: It was presumptuous of him to assume he would be chosen as team captain.

definition: Put an end to; suppress; reject or void by legal procedure.
example: The principal acted quickly to quash the rumour before it spread further.

definition: A traitor who collaborates with an enemy force occupying their country.
example: The story of the quisling who betrayed his own people became a symbol of cowardice.

definition: Exceedingly idealistic; unrealistic and impractical.
example: Her quixotic plan to cycle around the world in a week seemed impossible to everyone.

definition: Indicating mild or amused puzzlement; questioning.
example: The dog gave a quizzical tilt of its head when it heard the strange sound.

definition: A witty remark; a clever or sarcastic observation.
example: He always had a clever quip ready to lighten the mood in tense situations.

definition: Military observation of a region to locate an enemy or ascertain strategic features.
example: Before the mission, the soldiers conducted a careful reconnaissance of the area.

definition: Extremely hungry; very eager for gratification.
example: After the long hike, the children were ravenous and devoured their sandwiches.

definition: A story told about a past event remembered by the narrator.
example: The reunion was full of reminiscence as old classmates recalled their school days.

definition: Voluntarily cease to keep or claim; give up.
example: She was reluctant to relinquish her role as team captain.

definition: Lacking care or attention to duty; negligent.
example: It would be remiss of me not to thank all the volunteers for their hard work.

definition: The art of effective or persuasive speaking or writing.
example: The politician's speech was full of rhetoric but short on concrete solutions.

definition: A person who engages in sabotage; one who deliberately destroys or obstructs something.
example: The race team discovered a saboteur had tampered with the engine the night before.

definition: A profoundly wise person; an aromatic plant used in cookery; having or showing wisdom.
example: She sought the advice of the village sage before making her final decision.

definition: A raised wooden platform used for execution; a temporary structure used in building.
example: Workmen stood on the scaffold to repaint the upper floors of the building.

definition: Run hurriedly with short quick steps; deliberately sink one's own ship.
example: The little crab scuttled sideways across the rock and hid under a shell.

definition: Neat in dress and appearance; make someone look neat; a widespread coniferous tree.
example: She spruced herself up before heading to the interview.

definition: The state or situation of being alone; a lonely or uninhabited place.
example: He loved the solitude of early morning walks before the city woke up.

definition: Heavy, filling, and unappetising; dull and uninspired.
example: The stodgy lecture made half the class fall asleep.

definition: In music, with each sound sharply detached or separated; performed in an abrupt manner.
example: The drumbeat was staccato, each tap crisp and separate from the next.

definition: The quality or fact of being very determined; the quality of holding fast.
example: Her tenacity in practising every day finally led her to win the championship.

definition: Prevent from accomplishing something; oppose successfully.
example: The security guard managed to thwart the thief's plan to steal the painting.

definition: The state of being tedious; boredom; monotonous quality.
example: To escape the tedium of the long journey, she read her favourite novel.

definition: Perceptible by touch; clear and definite; real.
example: The teacher asked for tangible proof, not just a verbal claim.

definition: Of or relating to drama and the theatre; an actor or actress.
example: The school's most dedicated thespian had a lead role in every annual play.

definition: Speak badly of or tell lies about so as to damage someone's reputation.
example: He was furious that his rival had traduced him in front of the entire committee.

definition: Swollen; inflated.
example: After the bee sting, his hand became tumid and painful.

definition: Attack or kill by choking or strangling; control the flow of fuel or steam to an engine.
example: The mechanic adjusted the throttle to give the engine more power.

definition: Using money and other resources carefully and not wastefully.
example: By being thrifty with her pocket money, she saved enough to buy a new bicycle.

definition: A mean or miserly person; one who is reluctant to spend money.
example: He was such a tightwad he refused to buy birthday presents for anyone.

definition: Attempt to sell something, typically in an aggressive manner.
example: Street vendors were touting fresh fruit to passing shoppers.

definition: Offence or annoyance; shade or shadow, especially as cast by trees.
example: She took umbrage at the suggestion that she could not handle the challenge.

definition: Having an untidy or dishevelled appearance; not groomed.
example: After camping for a week, the hikers returned looking rather unkempt.

definition: Not pretentious or arrogant; modest.
example: Despite being one of the best students in school, she was quiet and unassuming.

definition: Not faltering; remaining strong and steady.
example: Her unfaltering belief in herself helped her push through the hardest training.

definition: Not feigned; genuine; sincere.
example: Her unfeigned joy when she passed her exams made everyone smile.

definition: Not aware of the full facts; unintentional.
example: She became an unwitting accomplice in the prank simply by holding the bag.

definition: Not able to be maintained or defended against attack or objection.
example: The coach's position became untenable after the team lost ten games in a row.

definition: Find fault with or scold persistently.
example: The teacher gently but firmly upbraided the student for repeatedly missing deadlines.

definition: Provoking loud laughter; very funny; making or characterised by a loud noise.
example: The comedian's uproarious performance had the entire crowd in stitches.

definition: Take a position of power or importance illegally or by force.
example: The ambitious general plotted to usurp the king's throne.

definition: Waver between different opinions or actions; be indecisive.
example: He vacillated for weeks before finally choosing which university to attend.

definition: A person without a settled home or regular work who wanders from place to place.
example: The charity provided hot meals to vagrants sleeping rough in the city.

definition: Live or spend time in a dull, inactive, unchallenging way.
example: After the exams she just wanted to vegetate on the sofa and watch films.

definition: Showing strong feeling; forceful, passionate, or intense.
example: She made a vehement objection to the unfair rule change.

definition: Of or relating to a fox; crafty; cunning.
example: His vulpine smile suggested he already knew the outcome of the negotiation.

definition: Green with grass or other rich vegetation; of the bright green colour of lush grass.
example: After weeks of rain, the hillsides were verdant and beautiful.

definition: To grant or give something to someone in a gracious manner.
example: The queen vouchsafed the knight an audience to hear his request.

definition: Making a loud and forceful sound; insistent and forceful in speech.
example: The crowd was vociferous in protesting the unfair decision.

definition: A trace or remnant of something that is disappearing or no longer exists.
example: Only a vestige of the ancient wall remained after centuries of erosion.

definition: Offering nothing that is stimulating or challenging; dull or uninteresting.
example: The movie received poor reviews for its vapid plot and forgettable characters.

definition: Give a slight involuntary grimace or shrinking movement from pain or distress.
example: She winced when the nurse gave her the injection.

definition: A smell that is only smelt briefly or faintly; a trace of something.
example: She caught a whiff of fresh bread as she walked past the bakery.

definition: Roll about or lie in mud or water; indulge in an unrestrained way in something.
example: After the disappointing result, she allowed herself to wallow in self-pity for just one day.

definition: Pale and giving the impression of illness or exhaustion.
example: After a week of illness, she appeared wan and tired at school.

definition: Sing in a gentle, pleasant way with variations in pitch; a gentle trilling sound.
example: A small robin warbled cheerfully in the apple tree outside the window.

definition: A network of interconnecting rabbit burrows; a densely populated or labyrinthine district.
example: The old city centre was a warren of narrow streets and hidden courtyards.

definition: Blow a current of air through grain to remove the chaff; remove unwanted elements.
example: The editor had to winnow hundreds of applications down to just ten.

definition: Attractive or appealing in a fresh, innocent way.
example: Her winsome smile won over the grumpy shopkeeper instantly.

definition: Become dry and shrivelled; cause to feel worthless; decline.
example: Without enough sunlight, the plants began to wither.

definition: A witty remark.
example: The teacher's unexpected witticism made the class burst out laughing.

definition: Lean and wrinkled, especially through age; shrivelled.
example: The wizened old fisherman had spent fifty years in the sun and wind.

definition: Have a long, complicated dispute; a dispute of this kind.
example: The two neighbours wrangled for years over the position of their shared fence.

definition: Characterised by, expressive of, or causing sorrow or misery; very bad; deplorable.
example: Her woeful expression told everyone she had just heard terrible news.

definition: Dislike of or prejudice against people from other countries.
example: The school worked hard to tackle xenophobia and promote respect for all cultures.

definition: A piece of rock within an igneous rock that is of a different origin.
example: Geologists examined the xenolith to understand how the volcanic rock had formed.

definition: A salt or ester of xanthic acid; used chiefly in ore-flotation processes.
example: Xanthate is an important chemical used in mining to separate valuable minerals from ore.

definition: Originating in a dry habitat; relating to an ecological succession beginning on dry ground.
example: The xerarch succession showed how plants slowly colonised the dry rocky hillside.

definition: At some distance in the direction indicated; over there.
example: The old farmer pointed to the hills yonder and said that was where he grew up.

definition: An unsophisticated country person; a rustic.
example: The city visitors laughed at themselves for looking like yokels in the busy market.

definition: The Christmas festival; the period immediately before and after Christmas Day.
example: The whole family gathered to celebrate Yule with singing and a feast.

definition: A well-paid young middle-class professional who works in a city job.
example: The cafe was popular with yuppies who liked to work on their laptops over expensive coffee.

definition: An exclamation used to express excitement, approval, or surprise.
example: Yowza! That was the most incredible goal I have ever seen!

definition: The time at which something is most powerful or successful; the point directly above an observer.
example: At the zenith of her career, she had won three world championships.

definition: A person who is fanatical and uncompromising in pursuit of their religious, political, or other ideals.
example: A zealot for recycling, he would retrieve cans from the bin to sort them correctly.

definition: Great enthusiasm and energy; the outer coloured rind of a citrus fruit used for flavouring.
example: She approached every challenge with the same infectious zest for life.
